---
title: "Subsampling Stability & Sample Size Optimization for Spatial Material Measurements"
author: "J. W."
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(ggExtra)
library(gridExtra)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# ==============================================================================
# PURPOSE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Evaluate whether n = 15 samples per specimen (SpecimenID) is under- or over-
# sampling. The analysis resamples subsets
# of samplees (n = 1…N_MAX) WITHOUT replacement, estimates the distribution of
# the subsample mean at each n, and determines the smallest n that achieves a
# practical notion of "stability".
#
# WHAT THE CODE DOES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Robustly resolves column names (handles sanitized headers and past renames).
# 2) Computes within-specimen CV% and sets data-driven stability targets:
#       - STABILITY_PCT : allowable % deviation of subsample mean from the
#                         full-sample (reference) mean.
#       - CI_HALF_PCT   : allowable % 95% CI half-width (precision target),
#                         expressed as % of the reference mean.
#       - DEV_Q         : deviation quantile used for the stability test
#                         (risk tolerance; e.g., 0.95 = 95th percentile).
#    Targets are chosen from tiers based on median within-specimen CV:
#       CV ≤ 5%   →  STABILITY_PCT = 2%,  CI_HALF_PCT = 2%,  DEV_Q = 0.95
#       5%<CV≤10% →  STABILITY_PCT = 3%,  CI_HALF_PCT = 3%,  DEV_Q = 0.95
#       CV > 10%  →  STABILITY_PCT = 5%,  CI_HALF_PCT = 5%,  DEV_Q = 0.90
#    (If CV can’t be computed, defaults to 3%, 3%, 0.95.)
#
# 3) For each SpecimenID, builds a "stability curve" by resampling n = 1…N_MAX:
#       - mean_est : mean of bootstrapped subsample means (for plotting).
#       - ci_low / ci_high : 95% bootstrap CI for the subsample mean.
#       - dev_qpct : DEV_Q-quantile of |subsample mean − reference mean|
#                    as a % of the reference mean.
#
# 4) Determines per-specimen "stable n" under two criteria:
#       - stable_n_relchange_q : smallest n where dev_qpct ≤ STABILITY_PCT.
#       - stable_n_ciwidth     : smallest n where CI half-width ≤ CI_HALF_PCT
#                                of the reference mean.
#
# KEY OUTPUTS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# - `stable_table` (per specimen):
#       SpecimenID | ref_mean | stable_n_relchange_q | stable_n_ciwidth
# - `summary_counts` (rollup for decision-making):
#       criterion | median_stable_n | pct_specimens_met_by_n15
# - Plots:
#   (A) Convergence plot: mean_est vs n with 95% CI ribbon and dashed ref_mean.
#   (B) Deviation quantile plot: dev_qpct vs n with STABILITY_PCT reference line.
#   (C) CI half-width plot: ci_half_rel vs n with CI_HALF_PCT reference line.
#
# HOW TO INTERPRET
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) stable_n_relchange_q (bias control):
#    - If most specimens achieve stable_n_relchange_q << 15, your current n = 15
#      is likely over-sampling for controlling mean bias at DEV_Q.
#    - If many specimens need n close to N_MAX to meet STABILITY_PCT, your n = 15
#      may be necessary (or even low if you tighten thresholds).
#
# 2) stable_n_ciwidth (precision control):
#    - This reflects the sampling needed to get a narrow 95% CI on the mean.
#    - If stable_n_ciwidth ~ 15 for many specimens, reducing n will widen CIs
#      beyond your target; either accept larger CI_HALF_PCT or keep n = 15+.
#
# 3) Tuning thresholds:
#    - STABILITY_PCT and CI_HALF_PCT should align with the *decision context*:
#      screening (lenient), development (moderate), release/QA (strict).
#    - Use DEV_Q to encode risk tolerance (0.90 = lenient, 0.95 = balanced,
#      0.975–0.99 = conservative).
#
# RULES OF THUMB
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# - Biological materials often show CV = 5–15%. Requiring 1–2% CI half-width
#   typically implies n larger than 15 unless CV is very low.
# - If your median CV drops after process improvements (thickness control,
#   moisture normalization, position balancing), you can revisit and tighten
#   STABILITY_PCT / CI_HALF_PCT or reduce n.
#
# DIAGNOSTICS & EDGE CASES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# - Specimens with <2 valid samplees are excluded from CV calculation.
# - Non-positive or missing means are filtered out for CV computation.
# - If column names change, the resolver will try fuzzy matching; otherwise it
#   fails fast with a helpful error listing available columns.
#
# PERFORMANCE TIPS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# - Set N_BOOT ~ 300–500 for faster, exploratory runs; 1000–2000 for tighter CIs.
# - If many specimens exist, consider running on a subset first, or parallelize
#   replicate generation if available in your environment.
#
# DECISION GUIDANCE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# - Over-sampling signal: median(stable_n_relchange_q) and median(stable_n_ciwidth)
#   both well below 15 → consider reducing n.
# - Borderline sufficiency: one criterion << 15 but the other ~ 15 → decide which
#   risk you care more about (bias vs precision) and set n accordingly.
# - Under-sampling: both criteria ≈ 15 or > N_MAX → increase n or relax targets
#   (or reduce process noise) to meet decision-quality requirements.
# ==============================================================================

# Decomposition Analysis #
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# ---- Parameters & data-driven targets (robust to sanitized column names) ----
N_BOOT  <- 1000
N_MAX   <- 15
FEATURE <- "density" #define feature (synthetic dataset)
ID_COL  <- "specimen_id" #define specimen ID (synthetic dataset)
DATA_PATH <- "synthetic_sheet_sampling_20x15.csv" # path to synthetic data
set.seed(123)

# Load data (use existing `dat` if present; otherwise read from CSV)
if (!exists("dat")) {
  # prefer unsanitized names; fall back to default if needed
  dat <- tryCatch(
    read.csv(DATA_PATH, check.names = FALSE),
    error = function(e) read.csv(DATA_PATH, check.names = TRUE)
  )
}

# --- Helper to find a column ignoring punctuation/case -----------------------
canon <- function(x) tolower(gsub("[^a-z0-9]", "", x))
find_col <- function(df, target_label) {
  cn  <- names(df)
  ccn <- canon(cn)
  tgt <- canon(target_label)
  hit <- which(ccn == tgt)
  if (length(hit) == 1) return(cn[hit])
  # try partial match if exact canonical not found
  hit <- grep(tgt, ccn, fixed = TRUE)
  if (length(hit) == 1) return(cn[hit])
  stop(sprintf(
    "Could not locate column '%s'. Available columns:\n  %s",
    target_label, paste(cn, collapse = ", ")
  ))
}

# Resolve actual column names in `dat`
feature_col <- find_col(dat, FEATURE)
id_col      <- find_col(dat, ID_COL)

# Coerce numeric feature and rename ID column to SpecimenID
dat[[feature_col]] <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dat[[feature_col]]))
names(dat)[names(dat) == id_col] <- "SpecimenID"

# Compute within-specimen CV (%) using available replicates
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr))
cv_specimen <- dat %>%
  group_by(SpecimenID) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_val = mean(.data[[feature_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val   = sd(.data[[feature_col]],   na.rm = TRUE),
    n_non_na = sum(!is.na(.data[[feature_col]])),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_non_na >= 2, is.finite(mean_val), mean_val > 0) %>%
  mutate(cv_pct = 100 * sd_val / mean_val)

med_cv <- median(cv_specimen$cv_pct, na.rm = TRUE)

# Set targets based on observed variability (practical tiers)
if (is.na(med_cv)) {
  STABILITY_PCT <- 3; CI_HALF_PCT <- 3; DEV_Q <- 0.95
} else if (med_cv <= 5) {
  STABILITY_PCT <- 2; CI_HALF_PCT <- 2; DEV_Q <- 0.95
} else if (med_cv <= 10) {
  STABILITY_PCT <- 3; CI_HALF_PCT <- 3; DEV_Q <- 0.95
} else {
  STABILITY_PCT <- 5; CI_HALF_PCT <- 5; DEV_Q <- 0.90
}

message(sprintf(
  "Median within-specimen CV = %.2f%%  ->  STABILITY_PCT = %s%%, CI_HALF_PCT = %s%%, DEV_Q = %.3f\n(feature_col = '%s', id_col mapped to 'SpecimenID')",
  med_cv, STABILITY_PCT, CI_HALF_PCT, DEV_Q, feature_col
))
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ---- Load & clean (same spirit as before) -----------------------------------
raw <- read.csv(DATA_PATH, check.names = FALSE)

# -- Compatibility shim for synthetic dataset --------------------------
if (!"Sample_ID" %in% names(raw)) raw$Sample_ID <- raw[[ID_COL]]
if (!"Rep" %in% names(raw) && "position_id" %in% names(raw)) raw$Rep <- raw$position_id


dat <- raw %>%
  dplyr::select(Sample_ID, all_of(ID_COL), Rep, all_of(FEATURE)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Rep = suppressWarnings(as.integer(Rep)),
    `{FEATURE}` := suppressWarnings(as.numeric(.data[[FEATURE]]))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::rename(DryMass = !!FEATURE, SpecimenID = !!ID_COL) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(DryMass))
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# ---- Resampling function with deviation quantile ----------------------------
# For each n, we draw many subsamples WITHOUT replacement, compute their means,
# then summarize:
#   - mean_est (for visualization)
#   - 95% bootstrap CI
#   - dev_qpct: the DEV_Q quantile of |mean - ref_mean| / ref_mean * 100
stability_curve_one <- function(x, ref_mean, n_max = N_MAX, n_boot = N_BOOT, dev_q = DEV_Q) {
  n_max_specimen <- min(length(x), n_max)
  ns <- 1:n_max_specimen

  purrr::map_dfr(ns, function(i) {
    boot_means <- replicate(n_boot, mean(sample(x, size = i, replace = FALSE)))
    dev_abs_pct <- abs(boot_means - ref_mean) / ref_mean * 100

    tibble::tibble(
      n        = i,
      mean_est = mean(boot_means),
      ci_low   = stats::quantile(boot_means, 0.025, names = FALSE),
      ci_high  = stats::quantile(boot_means, 0.975, names = FALSE),
      dev_qpct = stats::quantile(dev_abs_pct, dev_q, names = FALSE)
    )
  })
}
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# ---- Build curves for every specimen -------------------------------------------
stab <- dat %>%
  dplyr::group_by(SpecimenID) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(ref_mean = mean(DryMass), vals = list(DryMass), .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(curve = purrr::map2(vals, ref_mean,
                                    ~ stability_curve_one(.x, ref_mean = .y,
                                                         n_max = N_MAX, n_boot = N_BOOT, dev_q = DEV_Q))) %>%
  dplyr::select(-vals) %>%
  tidyr::unnest(curve) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    ci_halfwidth = (ci_high - ci_low) / 2,
    ci_half_rel  = ci_halfwidth / ref_mean * 100
  )
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# ---- Determine stable n under two criteria ----------------------------------
# 1) Deviation quantile criterion: dev_qpct <= STABILITY_PCT
# 2) CI half-width criterion:      ci_half_rel <= CI_HALF_PCT
stable_table <- stab %>%
  dplyr::group_by(SpecimenID, ref_mean) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    stable_n_relchange_q = {
      idx <- which(dev_qpct <= STABILITY_PCT)
      if (length(idx)) min(n[idx]) else NA_integer_
    },
    stable_n_ciwidth = {
      idx <- which(ci_half_rel <= CI_HALF_PCT)
      if (length(idx)) min(n[idx]) else NA_integer_
    },
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(SpecimenID)

stable_table
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# ---- Quick rollup for decision-making ---------------------------------------
summary_counts <- tibble::tibble(
  criterion = c(paste0(DEV_Q*100, "th pct abs deviation ≤ ", STABILITY_PCT, "%"),
                paste0("CI half-width ≤ ", CI_HALF_PCT, "%")),
  median_stable_n = c(stats::median(stable_table$stable_n_relchange_q, na.rm = TRUE),
                      stats::median(stable_table$stable_n_ciwidth,     na.rm = TRUE)),
  pct_specimens_met_by_n15 = c(
    mean(stable_table$stable_n_relchange_q <= 15, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    mean(stable_table$stable_n_ciwidth     <= 15, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  )
)

summary_counts
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Convergence of mean with 95% CI (same idea as before)
ggplot2::ggplot(stab, ggplot2::aes(x = n, y = mean_est)) +
  ggplot2::geom_ribbon(ggplot2::aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high), alpha = 0.15) +
  ggplot2::geom_line(size = 0.7) +
  ggplot2::geom_hline(ggplot2::aes(yintercept = ref_mean), linetype = "dashed") +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ SpecimenID, scales = "free_y") +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Convergence of Mean Sample Dry Mass (g) vs. Sampling Depth (n)",
    subtitle = "Ribbon = 95% bootstrap CI of subsample means; dashed = full-sample specimen mean",
    x = "Sample size (n)", y = "Mean estimate (g)"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal()
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# NEW: Deviation quantile vs n (the driver of stable_n_relchange_q)
ggplot2::ggplot(stab, ggplot2::aes(x = n, y = dev_qpct, group = SpecimenID, color = SpecimenID)) +
  ggplot2::geom_line() +
  ggplot2::geom_point(size = 1) +
  ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = STABILITY_PCT, linetype = "dotted") +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = paste0(round(DEV_Q*100), "th Percentile Absolute Deviation from Ref. Mean"),
    subtitle = paste0("Stability when deviation ≤ ", STABILITY_PCT, "%"),
    x = "Sample size (n)", y = "Deviation (%)"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "none")
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# CI half-width vs n (as % of ref mean) stays the same
ggplot2::ggplot(stab, ggplot2::aes(x = n, y = ci_half_rel, group = SpecimenID, color = SpecimenID)) +
  ggplot2::geom_line() +
  ggplot2::geom_point(size = 1) +
  ggplot2::geom_hline(yintercept = CI_HALF_PCT, linetype = "dotted") +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "95% CI Half-Width of Subsample Mean vs n",
    subtitle = paste0("Stability when CI half-width ≤ ", CI_HALF_PCT, "% of ref mean"),
    x = "Sample size (n)", y = "Percent of ref mean"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal() +
  ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "none")
```
